justification: All 7 required rules identified with correct invariant classification (9 rules total, exceeding minimum). Notification rule (BR-9) correctly marked invariant: false. Sanctions screening (BR-6) included as invariant: true. Timezone ambiguity for daily limit explicitly surfaced in both edge cases and low-confidence areas. 13 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level of 82 falls within the 60-85 acceptable range with thorough assumptions listed.

observations.text: All 7 expected rules present with correct invariant classifications, plus 2 additional rules (BR-4 for partially-verified limits, BR-8 for support-only unlock) that add depth
Notification rule (BR-9) correctly marked invariant: false with explicit note that 'failure to notify does not block the transaction'
Sanctions screening (BR-6) correctly identified as invariant: true with strong language ('no human override at the transaction level')
Timezone ambiguity surfaced explicitly in edge cases table and in low_confidence_areas and assumptions
13 acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format, far exceeding the 4 minimum, covering boundary conditions and edge cases
Correctly distinguishes partially-verified from unverified limits without conflating them
Identifies additional ambiguities beyond timezone: T+1 business days vs calendar days, partially-verified withdrawal rights, pre-settlement reversal allowance
Edge cases section covers race conditions on concurrent transactions, which is a sophisticated domain concern
Business impact assessment adds real-world context including OFAC fine ranges ($250K-$1M+)
Confidence level of 82 is slightly high given the number of open questions listed — 72-78 might better reflect the degree of ambiguity acknowledged

# Domain Analysis — Digital Wallet

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context
We are encoding the core rules of a **digital wallet** product — a regulated financial product that allows users to send, receive, and withdraw money. The business process spans identity verification (KYC), transaction processing, compliance (sanctions screening), security (account locking), and user notifications. This is a payments domain operating under financial regulatory obligations.

### Ubiquitous Language
